headline: CEO of GUROOH Ltd
summary:
Gideon is an OOH practitioner with over 30 years’ media experience working for both media owners and media agencies in Sales, Client Service and Audience Insight roles.

Current roles include, leading the Route Audience Analytics team for Ipsos in the UK; developing and promoting independent audience verification for DOOH globally with UniLED and as a Measurement Consultant for The World Out of Home Organization.

Previously, Gideon helped develop the UK’s OOH Audience currency, Route. Whilst as Chair of the Specialist IPAO body, he has worked to bring standards and regulation to the industry. His core experience is with the world’s largest buyer of OOH media, Kinetic, where he developed Audience Planning in the UK, across Europe, the US, and Asia, including the automation of OOH Planning and Transacting.

To keep him busy at home, Gideon has three daughters, plus dogs, cats, chickens, and some rather old cars.

experiences.title: Measurement Consultant
experiences.description: The World Out of Home Organization is the only global association for Out of Home businesses

experiences.title: Client Service Director
experiences.description: UniLED Software are the global leaders in DOOH ad serving, and independent play-out verification

experiences.title: Audience Activation Director
experiences.description: Supported Account teams and Client Service in Business Development and retention through bespoke data led planning and buying. Built client specific planning and implementation tools based on fusing client data with industry data. Tested and delivered data led optimisation of DOOH at the frame/hour level – increasing client value by 32% Developed and promoted Kinetic Products across DOOH and Mobile to introduce compliant fees as an alternative to the traditional commission based renumeration for the agency. Designed the next generation of Audience led planning and measurement tools for automated trading, introduced income migration techniques to increase retained margin from external users. Introduced Audience measurement and planning techniques in Thailand and India. Represented Kinetic data planning and UK OOH at international forums – WOOH, Russia, India, US, Thailand. Developed prohibition process and tools across the business to protect our clients. Adopted independent proof of play and copy trafficking for DOOH. Developed independent proof of audience delivery for DOOH with 3rd party supplier. IPAO – Member: 2014-2016 Represent Kinetic’s views at the OOH Specialist trade body. IPAO – Chair: 2016-2019 Led the OOH specialist agency trade body in reaching consensus on industry issues, represented the agencies’ collective view to other trade organisations. Developed and agreed the new Route funding formula for OOH specialists. Introduced oversight of the OOH Standards group, (the development of SPACE; Revenue reporting and analysis; the adoption of Open Direct and Extensions). Worked with the Advertising Association and Outsmart in developing the voluntary prohibition code for OOH to mirror OFCOM’s broadcast restrictions – worked with TfL to manage the world’s largest landlord led advertising prohibition.

experiences.title: Planning Development Director UK
experiences.description: ROUTE – 2011 to 2020.  Member of ROUTE Methodological committee, Delivery System Group and ROUTE Board. Delivering this cross industry research project within Kinetic and ensuring our market leading position is maintained and expanded. Ensuring implementation and usage across the business through a deep understanding of the data and delivering bespoke software for planners, buyers and media owners. Kinetic (Aureus) ROUTE software applications represent the industry gold standard in the UK and are used by major media owners, OoH media buying specialists, media auditors and by ROUTE itself. . London Olympics – 2010 to 2012 . Consultant role across Kinetic. Briefed and managed sponsor and non sponsor clients and agencies of process, LOCOG’s trading platform, relevant legalisation for media and copy over the Olympic period. working with LOCOG’s Brand Protection legal team,  Involved in numerous Kinetic/WPP publications and trade publications.

experiences.title: Business Director
experiences.description: MD of Signposter.com - A two year old SME media trading portal aquired by Kinetic in Jan 2010. Business analysis and plan post acquisition. 100% turnover growth in 6 months and a reduction of 75% in running costs through the introduction of new clients and catagories of advertiser.

experiences.title: Business Development UK
experiences.description: Working across all GroupM agencies and associated clients, overseeing two Group Account Directors and their teams. Adoption of best practice and process across teams, reducing the duplication of work and increasing service output. Client service at board level, developing new revenue models whilst maximising existing revenue.

experiences.title: Group Account Director
experiences.description: Managing an account handling team of 8 responsible primarily for mec, BJK&E (MAXUS), and S&N (Heineken). Business planning, budget and income forecasting, staff training, salary negotiation, account management at senior and board level across agencies and their associated clients.

experiences.title: Board Account Director
experiences.description: Account Director to Company Director in 9 months. Introduced commercialisation of the account team, negotiating larger client specific annual deals. Restructured account handling department as a whole, designed, developed and implemented consistent process management across planning and buying. Wrote and published first media planning guides for agencies and clients, seminars, ‘Outdoor Week’ takeovers of key agencies. Oversaw the adsorption and subsequent growth of acquired Tempus agency ‘Outdoor Focus’ and their retained account staff, in 2002

experiences.title: Agency Sales Director
experiences.description: Managing a team selling across the MO'F and Adshel portfolio to main London media agencies

experiences.title: Agency Sales Manager
experiences.description: Selling across the then Mills&Allen portfolio (now JCD Billboards) into London Media Agencies

experiences.title: Classified Sales Manager
experiences.description: Classified Telesales in a recession - really teaches you the meaning and rewards of hard work

education.description: Great City, wild time - plus I got an engineering degree and met my future wife

volunteer_work.title: Governor - Chair of Governors
volunteer_work.description: Local Authority Governor, Chairing the Finance and Resources Committee and the Governing Body at a 3 form entry primary school close to where I live. Being able to bring some of my commercial experience to the governance and oversight of the schools finances and procurement is incredibly rewarding. Partially from being able to give something back to the local community but mostly from being able to work with such an enthusiastic, hardworking and genuinely committed senior management team at a very well run school. I have learnt far more than I have offered, which I hope will put me in good stead to assist this and possibly other schools in the future.

volunteer_work.title: Treasurer of Local Scout Group
volunteer_work.description: I act as Treasurer of two local Scout Association Groups and also as an adult helper on an ad hoc basis. The treasurer's role is not a complicated one but working with committed Scout leaders has really shown me how much fun, experience and learning we can offer young people through Scouting. A very satisfying role where I can really see young people grow in confidence and skills - it also gives me the opportunity to enjoy the great outdoors and challenge myself with some of the activities that the Scout Association offers.
